flashcards.back: A data structure is a particular way of organizing, storing, and managing data in a computer so that it can be accessed and modified efficiently. It defines the relationship between data elements and the operations that can be performed on them.

flashcards.front: What is an Abstract Data Type (ADT)?
flashcards.back: An ADT is a mathematical model for data types where the data type is defined by its behavior (operations) from the point of view of a user, not by its implementation. Examples include List ADT, Stack ADT, and Queue ADT.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between primitive and non-primitive data structures?
flashcards.back: Primitive data structures are basic types directly operated upon by machine instructions (int, float, char, boolean). Non-primitive data structures are derived from primitive types and include linear structures (arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues) and non-linear structures (trees, graphs).

Big O notation describes the upper bound of an algorithm's time or space complexity as the input size grows. It expresses the worst-case growth rate, ignoring constants and lower-order terms. Common complexities: O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), O(n^2), O(2^n).

flashcards.front: What is the difference between linear and non-linear data structures?
flashcards.back: In linear data structures (arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues), elements are arranged sequentially and each element has a unique predecessor and successor. In non-linear structures (trees, graphs), elements are arranged hierarchically or in a network, and one element can connect to multiple others.

flashcards.front: What are the basic operations performed on data structures?
flashcards.back: The fundamental operations are: Traversal (visiting each element), Searching (finding an element), Insertion (adding a new element), Deletion (removing an element), Sorting (arranging elements in order), and Merging (combining two data structures into one).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of studying data structures?
flashcards.back: Studying data structures helps in understanding how to efficiently store and manage data, reducing time and space complexity, and improving the performance of algorithms.

flashcards.front: How do data structures affect algorithm efficiency?
flashcards.back: The choice of data structure can significantly impact the efficiency of an algorithm. Different data structures support different operations, and some operations may be more efficient in one data structure than another.
